body:
### Problem

(This was #39329 before I realized my config was not what I expected. This is the remaining odd behavior from that issue repackaged as its own bug.)

The initial(?) progress message does not get mapped to the expected place. In particular, I had a config that sent 'progress' to the pager, but it wasn't sent to the pager.

### Steps to reproduce

<details>

<summary>Reproducer config</summary>

```lua
vim.o.cmdheight = 0

-- Refer to |ui-messages|.
local targets_mapping = {
  msg = {
    '', -- Unknown (consider a |feature-request|)
  },
  cmd = {
  },
  pager = {
    'empty', -- Empty message (`:echo ""`), with empty `content`. Should clear messages sharing the 'cmdheight' area if it is the only message in a batch.
    'confirm', -- Message preceding a prompt (|:confirm|, |confirm()|, |inputlist()|, |z=|, …)
    'completion', --    |ins-completion-menu| message
    'search_cmd', -- Entered search command
    'search_count', -- Search count message ("S" flag of 'shortmess')
    'wildlist', -- 'wildmode' "list" message
    'bufwrite', -- |:write| message
    'echo', -- 	|:echo| message
    'echomsg', -- |:echomsg| message
    'lua_print', -- |print()| from |:lua| code
    'quickfix', -- Quickfix navigation message
    'shell_ret', -- |:!cmd| shell return code
    'undo', -- 	|:undo| and |:redo| message
    'wmsg', -- 	Warning ("search hit BOTTOM", |W10|, …)
    'progress', -- Progress message emitted by |nvim_echo()|
  },
}
local targets = {}
for target, msgs in pairs(targets_mapping) do
  for _, msg in ipairs(msgs) do
    targets[msg] = target
  end
end

require('vim._core.ui2').enable {
  enable = true,
  msg = {
    targets = targets,
  },
}

local function simulate_progress()
  local timer = vim.uv.new_timer()
  local progress = 0
  local token = 'simulated-task-id'
  local title = 'Reproducing Bug...'

  -- Start timer: 0ms initial delay, 200ms interval
  timer:start(
    0,
    200,
    vim.schedule_wrap(function()
      progress = progress + 5

      local is_done = progress >= 100
      local msg = is_done and 'Finished task' or ('Processing step ' .. progress)

      -- Matching your specific nvim_echo structure
      vim.api.nvim_echo({ { msg } }, false, {
        id = token,
        kind = 'progress',
        source = 'sim.provider',
        title = title,
        status = is_done and 'success' or 'running',
        percent = progress,
      })

      if is_done then
        timer:stop()
        timer:close()
      end
    end)
  )
end

simulate_progress()
```
</details>

Open vim with this config. The progress gets sent to msg rather than to the pager. If I move the `''` to output to pager it does work, so presumably the first progress event does not have the correct kind.

Notable: If `:` is pressed to enter command mode (or similar), the progress output starts going into the pager.

### Expected behavior

With this configuration, all progress updates should go into the pager.

### Nvim version (nvim -v)

NVIM v0.13.0-dev - commit fd2ea2742536a3bd7711190d94224008d7ca1e84

### Vim (not Nvim) behaves the same?

N/A

### Operating system/version

linux

### Terminal name/version

ghostty

### $TERM environment variable

xterm-ghostty

### Installation

Build from repo

comment_data.body:
It's recognizing your `'' -> msg` route for an empty `msg_show->trigger`. But configuring a route even for an empty `kind` is probably unnecessary, fine to just take the default target then:
```diff
diff --git a/runtime/lua/vim/_core/ui2/messages.lua b/runtime/lua/vim/_core/ui2/messages.lua
index fe8fe9c99f..36686646be 100644
--- a/runtime/lua/vim/_core/ui2/messages.lua
+++ b/runtime/lua/vim/_core/ui2/messages.lua
@@ -399,8 +399,8 @@ function M.msg_show(kind, content, replace_last, _, append, id, trigger)
     -- and seems useful enough even when the pager was entered manually.
     or (trigger == 'typed_cmd' and in_pager and fn.getcmdwintype() == '') and 'pager'
     -- Otherwise route to configured target: trigger takes precedence over kind.
-    or ui.cfg.msg.targets[trigger]
-    or ui.cfg.msg.targets[kind]
+    or (trigger ~= '' and ui.cfg.msg.targets[trigger])
+    or (kind ~= '' and ui.cfg.msg.targets[kind])
     or ui.cfg.msg.target
   if kind == 'search_cmd' and ui.cmdheight == 0 then
     -- Blocked by messaging() without ext_messages. TODO: look at other messaging() guards.
```

labels.description: Nvim 0.12 mesages/dialog/pager/notifications based on the UI-ext protocol
labels.name: ui2

title: ui2: The first 'progress' message seems to not get sent to the right destination
